page: Vultr voc-c-2c-4gb-50s-amd is an instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 2 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor with an x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 2 physical cores with 1 thread per core, operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz under a shared CPU allocation model. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is supported by one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data reveals a mixed performance profile. The instance demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the top tier (top 10%) and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression both scoring in the top 25%. However, multi-core performance is average, and the shared CPU allocation introduces limitations. Passmark CPU scores, database operations, and Redis SET operations (5.52 million operations per second) fall into the bottom 25%. Static web serving throughput is also limited, registering at 2.49 GB/sec, which places it in the bottom 10% of benchmarked servers.

Given its shared CPU architecture and 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio, this instance offers a budget-friendly entry point for workloads that require strong single-threaded execution but do not demand sustained, dedicated multi-core performance. It is suitable for lightweight application hosting, basic development environments, single-threaded utility scripts, and low-traffic web serving. It is less suited for high-throughput databases, intensive multi-threaded processing, or high-concurrency web applications due to its shared resource constraints and lower memory bandwidth.
description: Vultr voc-c-2c-4gb-50s-amd is an entry-level instance in the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 2 shared vCPUs on an AMD EPYC Genoa processor (x86_64 architecture) running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 4.0 GB of RAM and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Performance benchmarks show a stark contrast: single-core CPU performance is highly competitive, ranking in the top 10%, and single-threaded compression tasks perform strongly. However, the shared CPU allocation limits multi-core efficiency, resulting in below-average scores for database operations, Redis SET throughput, and static web serving. This resource profile makes the instance a cost-effective option for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, development environments, and low-traffic applications, while rendering it less suitable for resource-intensive database hosting or high-concurrency web services.
og_description: Vultr voc-c-2c-4gb-50s-amd is a shared-CPU instance featuring 2 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and 50 GB NVMe storage, offering strong single-core speed alongside budget-friendly resource sharing.
meta_description: Vultr voc-c-2c-4gb-50s-amd features 2 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and 50 GB NVMe SSD storage for budget-friendly, single-core workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly shared instance pairing strong single-core AMD EPYC performance with local NVMe storage for lightweight workloads.

4.0 GB of system memory and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong single-threaded compression
Shared CPU allocation resulting in lower multi-core and database throughput
Best suited for lightweight utility scripts, development, and low-traffic web serving
